summary: Market sentiment is cautiously optimistic but fragile, with equities staging record highs on ceasefire hopes even as geopolitical tensions between the US and Iran remain the dominant force shaping global risk appetite. A tentative 60-day US-Iran ceasefire extension is the central macro catalyst, lifting the S&P 500 and Nasdaq to record closing highs, though the deal remains contingent on Trump's approval and traders are skeptical a formal nuclear agreement will materialize this year. In equities, Arm Holdings surged to another all-time high on continued AI enthusiasm, Snowflake soared roughly 35% in its best day ever driven by AI-related demand, and Starbucks signaled early signs of a turnaround with improving afternoon traffic, while the FTSE 100 snapped a seven-day winning streak and European shares declined on Middle East uncertainty. The key risk factors investors are monitoring include the unconfirmed report of US aircraft destroyed at Iran's Bushehr facility, fresh US sanctions targeting Iranian military oil sales, a key US inflation measure posting its largest annual increase in three years, and the cascading impact of the Iran conflict on jet fuel trade routes and global energy markets. In the near term, the short-term outlook hinges almost entirely on whether Trump endorses the ceasefire deal, with confirmation likely triggering further equity gains, while any breakdown in negotiations or escalation of military activity would sharply reverse sentiment and pressure risk assets globally.
